texto: We wear the mask that grins and lies, It hides our cheeks and shades our eyes,— This debt we pay to human guile; With torn and bleeding hearts we smile, And mouth with myriad subtleties. Why should the world be over-wise, In counting all our tears and sighs? Nay, let them only see us, while We wear the mask. We smile, but, O great Christ, our cries To thee from tortured souls arise. We sing, but oh the clay is vile Beneath our feet, and long the mile; But let the world dream otherwise, We wear the mask!
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Paul Laurence Dunbar que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “We wear the mask that grins and lies, It hides our cheeks and shades our eyes,— This debt we pay …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, poem, smile, com foco em reflexao.

texto: In his moments of pride he had said all those things, half in fun and half in earnest, and he began to wonder how he could have been so many kinds of a fool for so long without realising it.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Laurence Dunbar sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “In his moments of pride he had said all those things, half in fun and half in earnest, and he beg…” articula tempo, pride, self-awareness em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como self-awareness.

texto: This, this indeed is to be accursed, For if we mortals love, or if we sing, We count our joys not by what we have, But by what kept us from that perfect thing.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Laurence Dunbar sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “This, this indeed is to be accursed, For if we mortals love, or if we sing, We count our joys not…” articula reflexao, poetry, indeed em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.

texto: A crust of bread and a corner to sleep in. A minute to smile and an hour to weep in. A pint of joy to a peck of trouble, And never a laugh but the moans come double. And that is life. A crust and a corner that makes love precious, With a smile to warm and tears to refresh us, And joy seems sweeter when cares come after, And a moan is the finest of foils for laughter. And that is life.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Laurence Dunbar sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “A crust of bread and a corner to sleep in. A minute to smile and an hour to weep in. A pint of jo…” articula vida, bread, hour em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como hour e joy.
